content:
# Luke Chapter 21 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA

This log records every verse where the three translators diverged in rendering, along with SOPHIA's resolution.

---

| Verse | Element | EMET (Literal) | LOGOS (Formal) | PNEUMA (Dynamic) | SOPHIA Resolution | Rationale |
|-------|---------|----------------|----------------|-------------------|-------------------|-----------|
| 1 | "the rich" | "the rich [ones]" | "the rich" | "wealthy people" | "the rich" | Greek substantive adjective; LOGOS is cleanest |
| 2 | PENICHRAN | "poor" | "poor" | "destitute" | "destitute" | PENICHROS is stronger than PTOCHOS (v.3); PNEUMA captures the distinction |
| 2 | LEPTA DUO | "two small-coins" | "two small copper coins" | "two tiny copper coins" | "two small copper coins" | LOGOS is accurate without unnecessary "tiny" |
| 3 | Formula | "Truly I say to you" | "Truly I say to you" | "I tell you the truth" | "Truly I say to you" | Preserves Lukan ALETHOS LEGO HYMIN formula |
| 3 | Verb | "cast-in more [than] all" | "cast in more than all of them" | "has put in more than all of them" | "put in more than all of them" | "put in" is natural; "of them" clarifies |
| 4 | HYSTEREMA | "her lack" | "her want" | "her poverty" | "her want" | "Want" is precise for HYSTEREMA; "poverty" could suggest PTOCHEIA |
| 4 | BIOS | "all the living [means]" | "all the livelihood" | "everything she had to live on" | "all the livelihood" | "Livelihood" best renders BIOS in one word |
| 5 | Speakers | "some were speaking" | "some were speaking" | "some of his disciples were remarking" | "some were speaking" | Greek TINON is indefinite; PNEUMA adds "disciples" without textual basis |
| 6 | Structure | Single sentence with brackets | Single sentence, clean | Split into two sentences, paraphrased | Single sentence (LOGOS) | Preserves the Greek structure and prophetic gravity |
| 7 | GINOMAI | "come-to-be" | "come to pass" | "take place" | "come to pass" | Traditional rendering with strong precedent |
| 8 | PLANAO | "led-astray" | "led astray" | "deceived" | "led astray" | PLANAO = "lead astray/cause to wander"; "deceived" would be APATAO |
| 8 | EGO EIMI | "'I am [he]'" | "'I am he'" | "'I am the one!'" | "'I am he'" | Preserves ambiguity of divine self-identification formula |
| 9 | AKATASTASIAS | "disorders" | "tumults" | "uprisings" | "tumults" | "Tumults" captures civic upheaval; "uprisings" implies organized revolt; "disorders" is flat |
| 10 | Verb tense | "he was saying" (imperfect) | "he said" | "he told them" | "he said" | Standard rendering; imperfect aspect not critical here |
| 11 | LOIMOI | "pestilences" | "pestilences" | "plagues" | "pestilences" | More literal; majority agreement |
| 11 | AP OURANOU | "from heaven" | "from heaven" | "from the sky" | "from heaven" | Biblical register; majority agreement |
| 12 | Structure | Brackets for implied objects | Clean participial phrases | Broken into separate sentences | Clean participial phrases (LOGOS) | Preserves Greek syntax without clutter |
| 13 | EIS MARTYRION | "unto a testimony" | "as a testimony" | "your opportunity to testify" | "as a testimony" | PNEUMA is too interpretive; LOGOS is natural |
| 14 | PROMELETAO | "practice-beforehand" | "practice beforehand" | "prepare your defense in advance" | "prepare beforehand" | Balances the meaning of PROMELETAO (rehearse/prepare) without awkwardness |
| 14 | THETE EN KARDIAIS | "Place [it] in your hearts" | "Set it in your hearts" | "make up your minds" | "Set it in your hearts" | PNEUMA too colloquial for this solemn command |
| 15 | ANTEIPEN | "speak-against" | "contradict" | "contradict" | "contradict" | Majority; better English for ANTEIPEN |
| 16 | SYNGENON | "relatives" | "kinsmen" | "relatives" | "kinsmen" | Formal register; captures kinship bond |
| 16 | PARADOTHESESTHE | "handed-over" | "delivered up" | "betrayed" | "delivered up" | Accurate rendering; "betrayed" is interpretive |
| 18 | OU ME construction | "will certainly not perish" | "shall by any means perish" (negated) | "not a single hair...will be lost" | "shall by any means perish" (negated) | Captures emphatic double negative OU ME |
| 18 | APOLETAI | "perish" | "perish" | "be lost" | "perish" | Majority; "perish" is stronger and more accurate |
| 19 | PSYCHAS | "souls" | "souls" | "lives" | "souls" | Preserves theological ambiguity of PSYCHE |
| 20 | STRATOPEDON | "encampments" | "armies" | "armies" | "armies" | Majority; STRATOPEDON by metonymy = armies in context |
| 21 | CHORAIS | "the regions" | "the country places" | "the countryside" | "the country places" | LOGOS is closest to the plural CHORAIS |
| 21 | AUTEN | "into her" | "into her" | "the city" | "into her" | Majority; preserves personification of Jerusalem |
| 22 | EKDIKESEOS | "vengeance" | "vengeance" | "punishment" | "vengeance" | Majority; EKDIKESIS includes vindication, not just penalty |
| 23 | OUAI | "Woe" | "Woe" | "How terrible it will be" | "Woe" | Majority; preserves prophetic register |
| 23 | THELAZOUSAIS | "those nursing" | "those who give suck" | "nursing babies" | "those who nurse" | Updated from LOGOS's archaic "give suck"; more dignified than PNEUMA |
| 24 | STOMATI MACHAIRES | "mouth of the sword" | "edge of the sword" | "edge of the sword" | "edge of the sword" | Majority; established English idiom for the Semitism |
| 24 | ETHNON (times of) | "nations" | "nations" | "Gentiles" | "nations" | Majority; preserves semantic range of ETHNON |
| 25 | ECHOS | "sound" | "roaring" | "roaring" | "roaring" | Majority; "roaring" better captures the force of ECHOS in context |
| 26 | OIKOUMENE | "inhabited-world" | "inhabited world" | "the world" | "inhabited world" | LOGOS/EMET more precise; distinguishes from KOSMOS |
| 28 | APOLYTROSIS | "redemption" | "redemption" | "deliverance" | "redemption" | Majority; retains ransom connotation |
| 28 | ENGIZEI | "draws-near" | "draws near" | "is drawing near" | "draws near" | Present tense ENGIZEI; simple present more accurate than periphrastic |
| 29 | IDETE | "See" | "Behold" | "Look at" | "Look at" | "Behold" is archaic; "Look at" is clear and carries imperative force |
| 30 | Rendering | "when they already sprout, seeing for yourselves" | "when they now put forth leaves, seeing it, you know of yourselves" | "When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves" | "when they now put forth leaves, you see for yourselves and know" | Combines LOGOS's "put forth leaves" with clearer syntax |
| 32 | OU ME | "will certainly not" | "shall not" | "will certainly not" | "shall by no means" | Strongest English rendering of the emphatic double negative |
| 33 | OU ME | "will certainly not pass-away" | "shall not pass away" | "will never pass away" | "shall by no means pass away" | Consistent with v. 32; emphatic double negative |
| 34 | KRAIPALH | "dissipation" | "dissipation" | "carousing" | "dissipation" | Majority; KRAIPALH = post-drinking stupor, not the revelry itself |
| 34 | MERIMNAIS BIOTIKAIS | "cares of life" | "cares of this life" | "anxieties of everyday life" | "cares of this life" | LOGOS adds "this" for clarity; "anxieties" is too modern |
| 35 | KATHEMENOUS | "sitting upon" | "who sit upon" | "who lives on" | "who dwell upon" | "Dwell" captures the sense of residing without overly literal "sit" |
| 36 | EN PANTI KAIRO | "in every season" | "at every season" | "at all times" | "at every season" | Preserves KAIROS (appointed time/season); "at all times" loses distinction |
| 37 | Structure | Overly literal word order | Balanced and clear | Adds "Jesus" and "hill" | LOGOS rendering | PNEUMA adds elements not in Greek; EMET too wooden |
| 37 | OROS | "mount" | "mount" | "hill" | "mount" | Majority; OROS = mountain/mount |
| 38 | ORTHRIZEN | "were rising-early" | "came early in the morning" | "would get up early in the morning" | "came early in the morning" | LOGOS is clear and direct |

---

### Summary Statistics

- **Total verses:** 38
- **Verses with significant divergence:** 33
- **Verses with full agreement (no adjudication needed):** 5 (vv. 11 partial, 17, 19 partial, 27, 31)
- **Resolutions favoring LOGOS:** 28 (primary basis)
- **Resolutions favoring EMET:** 3
- **Resolutions favoring PNEUMA:** 2 (v. 2 "destitute", v. 29 "Look at")
- **Composite resolutions (blending two or more):** 5

### Pattern Notes

LOGOS consistently provides the most balanced rendering between fidelity and readability, and consequently forms the backbone of the consensus text. EMET's hyper-literal approach (hyphenated compounds, bracketed supplements) is invaluable for understanding the Greek but produces English that is difficult to read as continuous text. PNEUMA's dynamic equivalence captures meaning effectively but frequently adds words not present in the Greek (e.g., "disciples" in v. 5, "Jesus" in v. 37), restructures sentences, and sometimes narrows theological terms (e.g., "punishment" for EKDIKESIS, "deliverance" for APOLYTROSIS). Where PNEUMA uniquely captures a nuance that the formal approaches miss — such as "destitute" for PENICHRAN in v. 2 — the consensus gratefully adopts it.

---

*Signed: SOPHIA*
*In council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
